title: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread

seo.title: How to Make Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread
easy Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread recipe
authentic Indian Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread
lowCompetitionKeywords: Indian Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread Recipe
h2Headings: What is Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread — a beloved Indian classic

description: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread is a beloved Indian flatbread, soft and chewy with a delightful garlic aroma, traditionally baked in a tandoor oven. This recipe helps you recreate its authentic taste and texture at home, perfect for scooping up curries or enjoying on its own. Its rich flavor and fluffy consistency make it an irresistible addition to any Indian meal.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the warm water, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. This indicates the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour and salt. Create a well in the center. Pour in the activated yeast mixture, yogurt, warm milk, and 1 tablespoon of melted ghee or butter. Mix with a spoon until a shaggy dough forms.

instructions.title: Knead and Rise
instructions.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 7-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Lightly grease a clean bowl with oil, place the dough inside, and turn to coat. Cover with a damp cloth or plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare Garlic Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, combine the minced garlic and chopped fresh cilantro with the remaining 1 tablespoon of melted ghee or butter in a small bowl. Set aside.

instructions.title: Shape the Naan
instructions.text: Once risen, gently punch down the dough and divide it into 6-8 equal portions. Roll each portion into an oval or teardrop shape, about 6-8 inches long and 1/4 inch thick. Brush one side of each naan with a little water.

instructions.title: Cook the Naan
instructions.text: Preheat a cast-iron skillet or heavy-bottomed pan over high heat until smoking. Alternatively, preheat your oven to 500F 260C with a pizza stone inside. Place a naan, water-side down, onto the hot skillet or pizza stone. Cook for 1-2 minutes until bubbles appear on the surface and the bottom is lightly charred. Flip the naan and cook for another 1-2 minutes until golden brown and cooked through. If using an oven, bake until puffed and lightly browned.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, transfer the naan to a plate. Immediately brush with the garlic-cilantro ghee mixture. Serve hot with your favorite Indian curries or as a side dish.

tips: For a truly authentic tandoor-like experience without a tandoor, use a very hot cast iron skillet or a preheated pizza stone in a screaming hot oven.
Do not over-knead the dough after the first rise, as this can make the naan tough. Handle it gently when shaping.
Serve naan immediately after cooking and brushing with ghee for the best flavor and softest texture. It pairs wonderfully with dal, curries, or tandoori chicken.
article: Garlic Naan Tandoor Bread is a beloved Indian Baking dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
